title: Energy Conservation
subtitle: Prioritizing rest and gentle routines
description: This phase may bring peak levels of nausea and fatigue. Conserving energy, maintaining hydration, and limiting physical strain become especially important. A slower, well-paced routine can help reduce discomfort and support daily stability.
what_to_expect: Nausea may peak and occur at multiple times during the day
Fatigue may feel more intense and persistent
Increased sensitivity to smells, motion, and environmental triggers
daily_safety_tips: Plan your day with minimal physical strain and include frequent rest breaks
Avoid sudden movements and rise slowly to prevent dizziness
Keep essentials within reach to reduce unnecessary movement
movement_guidelines: Limit activity to gentle movement like short walks or light tasks
Avoid prolonged standing, bending, or physically demanding work
Rest immediately when feeling tired, dizzy, or nauseous
environmental_awareness: Avoid strong odors, smoke, chemicals, and poorly ventilated areas
Stay in cool, quiet, and well-ventilated environments
Minimize exposure to crowded or noisy surroundings
travel_safety: Avoid unnecessary travel, especially during peak nausea periods
Use seatbelts correctly and sit in a comfortable, supported position
Take frequent pauses if travel is unavoidable
important_note: Reducing physical strain and conserving energy can help manage peak symptoms more effectively.
reassurance: Slowing down and prioritizing rest can help you feel more stable and comfortable.
medical_disclaimer: Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or modifying any physical activity.

diabetes.personalized_description: Balancing food intake, hydration, and minimal activity is important during peak nausea to maintain stable blood sugar levels.
movement_guidelines: Engage in very light movement such as short walks when comfortable
Monitor your body's response to both food and activity
diabetes.safety_focus: Watch for dizziness, sweating, or weakness, especially if appetite is reduced.
diabetes.reassurance: Small, consistent steps in routine can help maintain stability.

anemia.personalized_description: Increased fatigue may require more rest and very limited physical exertion.
movement_guidelines: Avoid physically demanding tasks and prioritize rest
Move slowly and carefully to prevent dizziness
anemia.safety_focus: Be aware of weakness, breathlessness, or lightheadedness.
anemia.reassurance: Extra rest and gentle pacing help support your energy levels.

hypertension.personalized_description: Maintaining a calm and low-stress environment is especially important during this phase.
movement_guidelines: Avoid stressful situations and physical exertion
Focus on slow, relaxed, and minimal movement
hypertension.safety_focus: Stay away from stress-inducing or physically demanding environments.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and controlled routine supports overall balance.

thyroid.personalized_description: Maintaining consistency and avoiding overexertion supports metabolic balance during peak symptoms.
movement_guidelines: Avoid both overexertion and prolonged inactivity
thyroid.safety_focus: Be mindful of unusual fatigue, restlessness, or fluctuations in energy.
thyroid.reassurance: Gentle and consistent routines help maintain stability.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: When managing multiple conditions, conserving energy and maintaining a gentle, structured routine becomes critical during this phase.
priority_guidelines: Avoid strenuous or physically exhausting activities
Limit movement to gentle and necessary tasks
Take frequent rest breaks and stay hydrated
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Careful energy management supports overall comfort and safety.
